text.title: Vendor Evaluation Scorecard for AI Tooling Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the vendor-evaluation-scorecard methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Scorecard MUST score every vendor on five dimensions: quality, cost, lock-in, security, SLA. Missing one is a fail.
rule.rationale: Vendors that win on a single axis (e.g. cost) often hide debt on another (lock-in, security); five-axis scoring surfaces tradeoffs.

rule.statement: Per-dimension weights MUST be set BEFORE scoring vendors, recorded in the scorecard.
rule.rationale: Adjusting weights after seeing scores is post-hoc rationalization; pre-commit removes this bias.

rule.statement: Each scorecard cell MUST cite ≥2 evidence items (doc link, benchmark, demo log).
rule.rationale: Single-source scores propagate vendor marketing as fact; cross-checking catches embellishment.

rule.statement: Lock-in score MUST include a quantified exit-cost (engineering days + data migration + index re-build).
rule.rationale: Unquantified lock-in is the most expensive mistake; forcing a number makes the bet visible.

rule.statement: If only one realistic vendor exists OR contract < $1k/yr, skip the scorecard.
rule.rationale: Rubric overhead exceeds decision value below those thresholds.
